truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 audit reveals only 2 of 20 true vulnerabilities were identified. The audited answer correctly found Command Injection via Config Loader and Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing, but missed 18 additional vulnerabilities including format string injection, integer overflow/underflow, use-after-free, race conditions, logic bombs, path traversal, weak PRNG, TOCTOU, unbounded recursion, and more. No false positives detected in the audited findings.

Items.Notes: Format-string injection via printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) at lines 237-239 where user-controlled config can inject format specifiers. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Integer overflow in factorial computation at lines 316-318 where result *= i uses long long which overflows for x >= 21. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.

Use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush at lines 817-826 where scratch pointer is enqueued to thread pool but memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() frees all blocks concurrently. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Run 2 correctly identified OS Command Injection via Config Loader with exact matching evidence from lines 564-568.

Run 2 correctly identified Buffer Overflow in Expression Evaluation at lines 831-835 where strcpy is used without null termination when expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_). This matches the stack buffer overflow vulnerability.
Race condition in ThreadSafeCounter at lines 482-487 where volatile long long value_ is read before lock_guard acquisition. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Logic bomb in authentication at lines 592-596 where login_attempts_ > 5 combined with EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE string bypasses authentication. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Path traversal in ConfigLoader at lines 542-544 where config_dir_ + filename is concatenated without validation. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Heap overflow in ExpressionParser at lines 366-370 where buffer_size_ = 256 but strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()) has no bounds check. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Hardcoded credentials at line 73 where ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH" is exposed in source. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Weak PRNG for session tokens at lines 636-640 where std::mt19937 with uniform_int_distribution(1000, 9999) produces only 9000 possible values. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Thread-unsafe timestamp cache at lines 230-232 where static std::tm cached_time is written without synchronization. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Insecure temp file handling at lines 624-633 where TEMP/TMPDIR is used to construct audit log path and system() executes shell commands. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.

Persistent authentication state at lines 578-590 where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across calls without proper session management. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Unhandled exception in variable parsing at lines 729-731 where std::stod(tokens[3]) can throw without try-catch. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Integer underflow in memory pool split at lines 132-134 where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow for unsigned size_t. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.

Unbounded recursion in parser at lines 382-433 where parse_expression calls parse_factor which can call parse_expression for parenthesized expressions with no depth limit. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Data race in result cache at lines 445-452 where result_cache_ is accessed without proper synchronization despite cache_mutex_ existing. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
TOCTOU in config loading at lines 542-547 where std::filesystem::exists is checked, then sleep_for(25ms), then std::ifstream opens the file. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.
Integer division by zero at lines 750-753 where 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length) can divide by zero if token_length is 0. Run 2 did not mention this vulnerability.

Items.Key: os command injection via admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5);\nsystem(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, potentially leading to full system compromise.|admin user executes 'exec <malicious_command>' where the command contains shell metacharacters|replace system() with a safe execution method. validate and sanitize all user input before execution.->os command injection via admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5);\nsystem(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, potentially leading to full system compromise.|admin user executes 'exec <malicious_command>' where the command contains shell metacharacters|replace system() with a safe execution method. validate and sanitize all user input before execution.
